00:02:717 (3,1) - I don't think it is that strong to be dashed; there is no feedback here. I mean sure it makes sense for structure but.. I believe walk is better for the sound.

Boom Boom Boom

00:57:045 (2,3) - Shouldn't this be dash like 00:56:149 (2,3) - or it's intentional for build-up?
00:54:806 (1) - I think making it flow would be more approchable

hi im godhacc
01:02:480 (1,2,3,4,1,1,1,2,3,4,5) - this played a little awkwardly, would do at least *something* to differentiate the 1/3 from the 1/4. making 01:02:788 (4) - active would also be mega swag

00:08:326 (4,5,6) - can we give these fellas some followpoints, matches intensity better and the other iterations of this section don't have any 0 spacing jumps when imo this one is the most intense one. maybe
00:17:711 (3,4) - ctrl+g rhythm sounds a bit more logical
00:33:865 (1) - think this'd be cooler as 2 circles, maybe like [this](https://nikolomara.s-ul.eu/bmbNVqMg.png)

01:01:249 (1,3,5) - make these symmetrical like 01:02:583 (2,2,2) - ? mostly cuz 01:01:711 (3) - felt a little off but i think doing large spacing triangle pattern into the small spacing triangle would be cool

01:18:634 (1,2,1,2,1,2,1,2,3,4) - this sounds super off, all the sliderends and circles have the strong sounds and held crashes while the sliderheads only have a vocal that ur ignoring everywhere else 

easiest solution is prob just delete 01:18:480 (6) - , move everything back 1/2 and make 01:20:634 (2) - a 1/1 slider which'd be similar to what cami does in their diff (and makes sense with the hs as well) but maybe u want to do something different
amazing

think it'd be nicer if 00:16:942 - this section had a *little* less density to serve as a proper cooldown section from how intense the intro is. it's also extremely similar to the next section rhythm wise (if anything its *more* intense since next section doesn't do any triples until the transition at the end) while the next section is clearly more intense so would fix that as well

for example 00:18:172 (5,6,7,8,9) - could do a rhythm like [this](https://nikolomara.s-ul.eu/VFzwkwk4.png)
01:08:018 (7,1) - could be cool to stack these like u did earlier 00:58:480 (10,1) -
01:11:403 (4,5) - we peaking here so i think unstacking these is better
00:20:634 (7,8,1,2,3) - this movement feels super awkward and lowkey 00:20:634 (7) - sounds better as a 2/1, would extend the slider and rearrange these to flow a little more smoothly

00:58:788 (1,2,3,4,5,6) - 01:08:634 (1,2,3,4,5,6) - imo normal diff players don't hear the contrast enough to warrant the difference in patterning, would make these consistent (personally prefer unstacked since this is the chorus)
01:11:095 (1) - would be nicer as a 1/1 slider imo

01:17:095 (5,6) - pulling this stack out of nowhere feels a little bs to new players, even if an easy diff exists imo. rhythm also feels off with this huge gap so would rather u unstacked this and added circles 01:17:865 - and 01:18:326 - for difficulty increase
00:36:634 (1,2,3) - personally not a big fan of overlapping these
01:15:095 (3) - making this a single circle to add a little break would be nice this is quite the chain

00:12:018 - soft finish sounded kinda off when rest of the section does normal finish, also normal whistle?
00:46:480 - nwh?
01:28:172 - don't think u need a finish here
